title: Lymania corallina
intro: Lymania corallina, also known as the Coral Lymania or Coral Air Plant, is a rare and endangered species of bromeliad, a type of epiphytic flowering plant native to Australia. The health benefits of Lymania corallina include its potential to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other secondary metabolites, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lymania corallina include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are traditionally used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and soothe skin irritations.
taxonomy: Lymania corallina is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. It is a type of epiphyte native to the subtropical regions of Australia. Within the genus Lymania, Lymania corallina is a distinct species characterized by its unique features. The plant is further classified as being part of the subfamily Tillandsioideae. This classification is based on its physical characteristics and genetic relationships with other bromeliads.
